content.full_text:
August 9, 1990

Ms. Alice Hughes  
Fair Political Practices Commission  
P.O. Box 807  
Sacramento, California 95804

Dear Ms. Hughes:

I am writing to request clarification of the need for specified members of the Commission on State Mandates (Commission) to file the Form 730, Statement of Economic Interests, with the Fair Political Practices Commission.

The Commission is composed of five members. Those members are the Director of Finance, the State Treasurer, the State Controller, the Director of the Office of Planning and Research and a Public Member. It is common practice for some of these members to send designees to Commission hearings. Occasionally, an individual designee will sit for only one hearing in a given fiscal year. At these hearings, the designees act in place of the statutorily named officer.

As I recall, in our earlier conversation, you stated that it is necessary for any voting designee of the Commission to file a Form 730. However, the statutory or regulatory authority governing designee filings does not appear to exist. Therefore, could you please outline the authority for requiring that a designee file a Form 730? Also, in such a case, would the designee need to file an Assuming Office Statement within 30 days of the Commission hearing, or some other date? Finally, if no other hearings are anticipated for that designee, would a Leaving Office Statement need to be filed within 30 days of the hearing?

If you have any questions, please feel free to contact me.

Sincerely,

[Signature]
ROBERT W. EICH  
Executive Director

RWE:cm:0485b
